title: What’s the most profitable EA you’ve actually used on MT4/MT5?
author: Annual-Register-3683
selftext:
I’ve been thinking about automated trading for a while, and I’ll admit I’ve wasted money on a few guaranteed profit bots that ended up being nothing but lessons.

The closest I’ve gotten to something reliable was when I built a really simple EA around a strategy I already traded manually. I ran it on a tradingfx VPS setup so it could execute while I was at work, and it actually held up pretty well. Nothing wild, but steady enough to keep me interested.

Now I’m curious about other people’s experiences. Has anyone here found an EA on MT4 or MT5 that stayed profitable over the long run? Like, live condition, not just backtests and demo hype. Would love to know what worked for you.

comments.author: EndlessKnight_154
comments.body:
I’ve been there too. I bought a couple of those guaranteed profit EAs when I started, and all they really did was teach me what to avoid. The only bots that ever made sense long-term were the ones I built off strategies I already traded manually, sounds like you’re heading in the same direction, which is good.

If you want to take it further, maybe pair the EA with prop firm accounts instead of putting your own capital on the line. You don’t need huge returns, just steady ones. I keep my setups running with cheapforexvps so everything stays up during London/NY while I’m busy, and it’s made the whole process way smoother.

comments.author: Careless-Diamond-277
comments.body: Forex fury is good,but I mostly Use my own ofc

comments.author: KD_Hub
comments.body: Most of the “profitable” EAs fall apart once market regimes shift - especially the ones optimized to death on backtests. The only ones I’ve seen hold up are rule-based systems built off a trader’s own edge, then enhanced with data filters (volatility, session bias, risk caps), which is basically where AI-style models help by adapting instead of curve-fitting. Long-term, automation works best as an execution + risk layer, not a magic signal machine.

comments.author: AttitudeGrouchy33
comments.body:
Most EAs I tested bombed in live markets because they were optimized on clean backtests. Market shifts killed them fast.



What worked better: starting with a super simple system I understood fully - like a basic moving average crossover. Then I added filters one by one (volatility, time of day, risk caps) and actually traded it small for months before scaling.



Biggest mistake was chasing complexity. My best EA ran on 3 rules and strict position sizing. Boring but consistent.



What's the simplest strategy you've gotten to work live?

comments.author: Amberkhell
comments.body: I have been using and testing EA's from the past 3 years, Actually you can call me an Algo Trader :) most of the EA will liquidate your account in 2-3 weeks. but it doesnt mean good trading bots does not exist. I have rarly find 2-3 in the past 3 years which has solid capital protection messures with stoplosses and keep the DD low. currently using SmartEdge Trading which works perfectly, it makes 10%+ monthly and consistantly, least in the past 7 months. The drawdown is low and they have stoplosses to secure the capital which is the best thing in my opinion.

comments.author: Slow_Exercise_7957
comments.body: I have one that has proven track record , i built it from my own trading strategy . The profit factor is 5 but at times it gets to 4.5 which is still very okay since its profitable . Its now 6 years down the line .
